input.headline: Millennials are set to inherit tons of their parents' stuff — whether they want it or not

raw.headline: Millennials are set to inherit tons of their parents' stuff — whether they want it or not

winner: My parents told me I'd inherit the earth someday, but now it seems all I'm getting is a pile of lawn furniture and an instruction manual on how to fold it properly.

candidates.text: My parents keep telling me I'm about to inherit a fortune... turns out, it's just all their old furniture.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on inheritance expectations, concise and specific with a relatable punchline."}

candidates.text: My parents told me I'd inherit the earth someday, but now it seems all I'm getting is a pile of lawn furniture and an instruction manual on how to fold it properly.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on inheritance expectations, specific and concise with a relatable punchline."}

candidates.text: When asked what he wanted from his inheritance, young Tim replied, "I never asked for these tonnes of tonnes, but I guess now I can finally build my own Tonka Empire!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific with a clever play on 'tonnes' and 'Tonka Empire,' which ties back to the headline's theme."}

candidates.text: Why did millennials invent the recycling bin? To ensure they could repurpose their parents' stuff, even if they didn't want it!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat relevant and has a mild twist, but the connection between millennials inventing recycling bins and inheriting parents' stuff feels forced and lacks specificity or surprise."}

candidates.text: Here you go: "Well, at least now we know where our future storage units are coming from.
